rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in the correct AADL scope: component type annexes for interface-level assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings.
Implementation-level signal routing contracts should be placed in the component implementation annex, not the type annex.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings must be placed in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed model.
Component type contracts may only reference features visible at the type scope; implementation-level references belong in the implementation annex.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Subcomponent ports should not be used in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes; this violates AADL scope rules.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the input AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Requiremnt analyst tasks and compact semantics
Model analyst tasks and AADL structural extraction
AGREE syntax notes for assume/guarantee blocks
